function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_integration_boot.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_integration_boot
description: >
  System-level integration test: full SoC boot of mspm33c321a_derivative.
  The CPU fetches the reset vector from XIP flash at 0x00000000, executes
  a pre-compiled firmware image (crt0 + main), and writes a magic signature
  word to a fixed SRAM address as a boot-complete indicator. The test
  monitors the SRAM location and declares PASS when the signature appears.
checklist_ids:
  - INT-01
test_names:
  - test_int_full_boot
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

flash_image:
  hex_file: verification/fw/xip_boot_smoke.hex
  base_addr: 0x00000000
  size_bytes: 0x00080000   # 512 KB XIP image

boot_signature:
  addr: 0x20000000
  value: 0xB007C0DE       # Magic value firmware writes on successful boot

external_devices: []

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: load_flash_image
    file: verification/fw/xip_boot_smoke.hex
    description: Pre-load XIP flash BFM with firmware image containing crt0 + main
  - step: 2
    action: assert_reset
    cycles: 20
    description: Assert reset for 20 cycles
  - step: 3
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset; CPU begins execution from 0x00000000
  - step: 4
    action: monitor_ifu_address
    expected: 0x00000000
    description: Confirm first instruction fetch is from 0x00000000 (reset vector)
  - step: 5
    action: poll_sram_signature
    addr: 0x20000000
    expected_value: 0xB007C0DE
    timeout_cycles: 1000000
    description: >
      Poll SRAM[0x20000000] for magic signature 0xB007C0DE. The firmware
      initialises BSS, runs main(), and writes the signature on completion.
  - step: 6
    action: verify_no_fault
    description: Confirm no AHB fault, XIP error, or CPU exception during boot sequence
  - step: 7
    action: verify_peripheral_accessible
    peripheral: UART0
    register: STATUS
    description: Confirm APB peripherals accessible after boot (UART0 STATUS readable)

checks:
  - id: CHK-INT-01
    checklist_id: INT-01
    description: SRAM signature 0xB007C0DE written by firmware within 1M clock cycles
    expected: SRAM[0x20000000] == 0xB007C0DE within 1,000,000 clock cycles
  - id: CHK-INT-01B
    checklist_id: INT-01
    description: First CPU instruction fetch is from XIP address 0x00000000
    expected: IFU address == 0x00000000 on first valid fetch
  - id: CHK-INT-01C
    checklist_id: INT-01
    description: No AHB HRESP errors or CPU exceptions during boot sequence
    expected: Clean execution; no fault signals asserted

pass_criteria:
  - CHK-INT-01, CHK-INT-01B, CHK-INT-01C all PASS
  - Simulation completes within 1,000,000 clock cycles
  - No X-states observed on critical data paths after reset release
"}
function.name: str_replace_editor

extras.new_content:
scenario_name: scenario_integration_boot
description: >
  System-level integration test: full SoC boot of mspm33c321a_derivative.
  The CPU fetches the reset vector from XIP flash at 0x00000000, executes
  a pre-compiled firmware image (crt0 + main), and writes a magic signature
  word to a fixed SRAM address as a boot-complete indicator. The test
  monitors the SRAM location and declares PASS when the signature appears.
checklist_ids:
  - INT-01
test_names:
  - test_int_full_boot
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

flash_image:
  hex_file: verification/fw/xip_boot_smoke.hex
  base_addr: 0x00000000
  size_bytes: 0x00080000   # 512 KB XIP image

boot_signature:
  addr: 0x20000000
  value: 0xB007C0DE       # Magic value firmware writes on successful boot

external_devices: []

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: load_flash_image
    file: verification/fw/xip_boot_smoke.hex
    description: Pre-load XIP flash BFM with firmware image containing crt0 + main
  - step: 2
    action: assert_reset
    cycles: 20
    description: Assert reset for 20 cycles
  - step: 3
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset; CPU begins execution from 0x00000000
  - step: 4
    action: monitor_ifu_address
    expected: 0x00000000
    description: Confirm first instruction fetch is from 0x00000000 (reset vector)
  - step: 5
    action: poll_sram_signature
    addr: 0x20000000
    expected_value: 0xB007C0DE
    timeout_cycles: 1000000
    description: >
      Poll SRAM[0x20000000] for magic signature 0xB007C0DE. The firmware
      initialises BSS, runs main(), and writes the signature on completion.
  - step: 6
    action: verify_no_fault
    description: Confirm no AHB fault, XIP error, or CPU exception during boot sequence
  - step: 7
    action: verify_peripheral_accessible
    peripheral: UART0
    register: STATUS
    description: Confirm APB peripherals accessible after boot (UART0 STATUS readable)

checks:
  - id: CHK-INT-01
    checklist_id: INT-01
    description: SRAM signature 0xB007C0DE written by firmware within 1M clock cycles
    expected: SRAM[0x20000000] == 0xB007C0DE within 1,000,000 clock cycles
  - id: CHK-INT-01B
    checklist_id: INT-01
    description: First CPU instruction fetch is from XIP address 0x00000000
    expected: IFU address == 0x00000000 on first valid fetch
  - id: CHK-INT-01C
    checklist_id: INT-01
    description: No AHB HRESP errors or CPU exceptions during boot sequence
    expected: Clean execution; no fault signals asserted

pass_criteria:
  - CHK-INT-01, CHK-INT-01B, CHK-INT-01C all PASS
  - Simulation completes within 1,000,000 clock cycles
  - No X-states observed on critical data paths after reset release
